attractions-manager:
## Attractions manager
- SSOC Code: 14322
- Major Group: 1 (Managers)
- Sector: Human Resource
- Median Monthly Wage: SGD 6,195
- Wage Range (P25-P75): SGD 4,300 – SGD 8,080

### Key Tasks (from SkillsFuture Skills Framework)
- Keep abreast of local and global HR trends and developments to provide further benchmarking insights on data analysis outcomes.
- Resolve data availability and data quality challenges with data cleansing techniques.
- Apply data governance concepts and principles to identify, collect and prepare data for analytics and HR metrics benchmarking.
- Develop insightful presentation derived from data analytics and HR metrics benchmarking using dashboards or data visualisation tools.
- Analyse financial and HR data by employing data mining, modelling, predictive analytics, and benchmarking tools and techniques to create insights and foresights to guide decision-making.
- Derive relevant insights from analysis and recommend enhancements to the organisation's HR practices taking into consideration the business context and operating environment.
- Correlate financial and HR data to design HR metrics, identify causal relationships, analyse trends, develop forecasts and projections, and draw insights and foresights for decision-making.
- Incorporate diversity and inclusion principles into HR policies and translate the policies into day-to-day practices at workplace.
- Plan and execute diversity and inclusion related activities that are compliant with diversity and inclusion policies and embrace diversity and inclusion culture.
- Contribute towards building the corporate branding for diversity and inclusion in all communications with stakeholders, respecting differences in perspectives and opinions, and working towards a mutually agreed outcome.
- Promote effective working relationships with people of diverse race, language, religion or cultures, embracing differences in perspectives, traditions and culture, and adopting an open mindset at all times.
- Calculate and analyse cost and headcount implications resulting from proposed strategic workforce plan and recruitment plan.
- Manage manpower cost and cost to hire within the approved budget.
- Propose, plan and execute HR initiatives that balance and meet the needs of the people, business or organisation.
- Develop annual recruitment plans for the purpose of filling resource gaps or redeploying resources based on skills.
- Align all employee lifecycle activities to the overall HR strategy and plan.
- Identify the data sources to gather and prepare relevant workforce data to perform workforce gap analysis.
- Collaborate with business leaders to model and refine the current workforce profile, and project the future skills demand and supply.
- Analyse strategic workforce planning information and advise business leaders on the range of resource planning options.
- Interpret the political, economic, social, technological, legal, environmental issues and assess the implications on strategic workforce planning for the organisation.
- Propose, plan and execute HR strategy and plan within the approved budget.
- Design recruitment and selection criteria with reference to CWFs and KTs and TSCs and GSCs of Skills Frameworks
- Leverage Critical Work Functions and Key Tasks (CWFs and KTs) of Skills Frameworks to develop employer branding collateral
- Develop competency-based interviews with reference to Technical and Generic Skills and Competencies (TSCs and GSCs) of Skills Frameworks
- Design competency assessment tools with reference to TSCs and GSCs of Skills Frameworks
- Develop job advertisements with reference to CWFs and KTs of Skills Frameworks
- Leverage CWFs and KTs and TSCs and GSCs of Skills Frameworks in developing onboarding programmes for new hires
- Develop behaviour-based interviews with reference to CWFs and KTs of Skills Frameworks
- Support line managers in drafting and issuing employment contracts, taking into account all relevant statutory terms and benefits.
- Communicate with employees on areas such as benefits and claims eligibility and administration, payroll deductions, retirement, re-employment and termination guidelines.
- Research and apply prevailing labour policies, employment laws and regulations, including but not limited to tripartism, employment laws for foreign nationals, Fair Consideration Framework,
- Manage internal stakeholders beyond the team and external stakeholders to achieve shared goals
- Provide coaching and advice to junior team members
- Justify the resources required to support changes in resources, procedures, systems, or technology within the function
- Manage team resources to ensure adequate staffing and capability levels
- Monitor the function's financial inflow and outflow against allocated budgets and forecasts
- Set individual objectives, periodically reviewing and assessing performance of direct reports
- Assess feasibility of proposals to improve internal workflows
- Translate the long-term objectives for the Talent Attraction function into tactical plans
- Gather feedback from new joiners about their onboarding experience so as to take proactive actions to address areas for improvement in a timely manner.
- Collaborate with business leaders to help new joiners assimilate into the organisation quickly.
- Facilitate assimilation of new joiners into the organisation and team through engagement with business units to understand business needs and job requirements.
- Design and deliver impactful orientation programmes for new joiners to facilitate their onboarding experience and help them to be familiarised with the organisation
- Adapt and tailor different styles and preferences when communicating to different audiences or stakeholder groups.
- Plan and execute communication activities using the appropriate channels/ tools for the targeted audience.
- Demonstrate empathy and respect when handling difficult conversations, dilemmas or paradoxes.
- Build trust (being a trusted advisor) through assisting key stakeholders in solving people related issues successfully.
- Establish credibility by gaining confidence of others through a demonstration of business and technical knowledge.
- Develop positive working relationships with people through strong inter-personal skills.
- Present and communicate with impact and empathy through clarity in messages, and engaging audience through active listening and inquiry.
- Develop communication material/ information kits for the HR programmes/ activities to convey desired messages clearly and effectively to the targeted audience.
- Design a unique and compelling employer brand and employee value proposition to establish a differentiated and distinctive organisational brand in the market.
- Put together offers of employment that takes into account internal and external compensation equity, and communicate offers of employment in a persuasive and professional manner to secure acceptance of employment offers.
- Articulate an employee value proposition that is simple to understand, differentiated from competition, and have a strong rational and emotive appeal to choice candidates.
- Communicate the employee value proposition to choice of candidates through letters of employment, verbal communication and persuasion, as well as leveraging on advocates from the business to reaffirm the employee value proposition.
- Advise hiring managers on the principles of fair and unbiased employment selection practices.
- Recommend and deploy a range assessment tools, techniques and technology solutions for hiring managers to evaluate the job-fit of candidates in order to reduce time taken to hire and manage hiring costs within the approved budget.
- Analyse all information gathered from the screening and assessment process to support hiring managers in making decisions on offers of employment.
- Design employer branding collateral by working with organisation's marketing and corporate communications team.
- Execute recruitment plans in collaboration with business leaders to ensure open positions are successfully filled.
- Recommend hiring standards, selection criteria and job requirements (knowledge, skills and experience) to fulfil the current and future business plans of the organisation.
- Develop a database of contacts to facilitate access to potential recruits of the organisation to allow proactive and immediate sourcing of qualified candidates when the need arises.
- Execute initiatives to deliver the employer branding strategy and promise through collaboration with internal and external stakeholders.
- Incorporate employee value proposition in all the employee lifecycle activities to deliver the employer brand promise.
- Implement and operate various aspects of the HR operating model to deliver HR services as set out in service management framework and in accordance with HR policies.
- Review the range of HR services against their corresponding HR service delivery channels critically to identify opportunities for continuous improvement of service quality or costs reduction.
- Implement HR transformation programmes to improve the effectiveness of HR service delivery and cost efficiency for the organisation.
- Adopt a service-oriented mindset and people-centricity in dealing with workforce and business stakeholders.

- Implement sensory testing methodologies to assess consumer acceptance for stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods
- Document prototype testing processes, results, and post-testing feedback from stratified consumer groups to finalise optimal stratified food product formulations and concepts
- Work with cross-functional teams to develop educational materials for targeted demographics that communicate the nutritional advantages of stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods
- Provide nutritional specifications to cross-functional teams to support the creation of informative content on stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods
- Work with cross-functional teams to engage diverse consumer groups on informed dietary choices based on the stratified nutrition and nutritional value of alternative and/or novel foods
- Collaborate with health professionals to disseminate information about the nutritional value of stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods
- Work with cross-functional teams to analyse consumer feedback on stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods to ensure educational materials and product offerings resonate with target demographics
- Advise product development teams on consumer nutrition trends and dietary patterns that could impact the future of stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods
- Identify novel ingredients that offer functional benefits, enhanced nutrition, and improved gut microbiome for stratified consumer groups with research findings
- Evaluate the feasibility of novel ingredients in delivering the desired nutritional value and nutrition bioavailability for stratified consumer groups, aligning to established dietary guidelines and market data
- Develop guidelines for selecting novel ingredients that align with product concepts for stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods, considering nutritional value, safety, and consumption trends
- Establish techniques for fortifying food products to ensure nutritional equivalency or advantage of stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods compared to conventional food products
- Assess the impact of different fortification methodologies and technologies on the quality, stability, and shelf-life of stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods
- Recommend modifications to novel ingredients and fortification methodologies to ensure stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods deliver on nutritional value
- Identify relevant local and international regulations related to product labelling, nutrition claims, and safety standards for stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods
- Develop precise nutrition fact panels and labels for new stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods to ensure transparency in nutrition declarations
- Work with compliance teams to conduct safety assessments for ingredients utilised in stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods to ensure compliance with food safety standards
- Work with quality assurance teams to audit formulations and fortification methodologies used for new stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods, resolving any quality issues identified
- Update nutrition fact panels and labels in response to formulation changes or evolving regulatory requirements to ensure continued compliance during manufacturing
- Uphold compliance of globally distributed stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods with international food safety and nutrition labelling standards
- Document audit, safety assessment, and compliance processes carried out on stratified food products and alternative and/or novel foods to facilitate data submissions and reporting
- Collaborate with legal and regulatory teams to review the health claims of stratified nutrition food products and alternative and/or novel foods, ensuring they meet legal and regulatory standards

personnelhuman-resource-officer:
## Personnel/Human resource officer
- SSOC Code: 24233
- Major Group: 2 (Professionals)
- Sector: Human Resource
- Median Monthly Wage: SGD 4,000
- Wage Range (P25-P75): SGD 3,370 – SGD 4,605

### Key Tasks (from SkillsFuture Skills Framework)
- Evaluate impact of HR policies and programmes on workforce performance taking into consideration of the priorities, industry, size and complexity of the organisation as well as risks, costs and benefits to the organisation
- Articulate the alignment and impact of HR strategy, goals and initiatives on workforce, business and financial performance.
- Deliver credible and persuasive presentations to senior business leaders and stakeholders and display deep understanding of the business and industry.
- Display professional maturity and executive presence in dealing with contentious or sensitive topics during discussion with senior business leaders and stakeholders.
- Advise senior business leaders and stakeholders to invest strategically on people related matters where it has high impact on current and future workforce performance.
- Lead the talent agenda by overseeing the development and strengthening of the talent bench strength and leadership pipeline in order to a build high performance workforce, meet succession needs and ensure business continuity and sustainability.
- Formulate and shape the organisation's business strategy and enterprise risk management with senior business leaders and stakeholders by giving inputs related to business and people agenda.
- Advise senior business leaders and stakeholders on the design of the organisation structure to enable business strategy and support the business objectives aligning to the organisation's vision, mission and goals.
- Understand and interpret business and financial performance metrics from people's perspective to identify key challenges and root causes and recommend strategic solutions related to workforce performance.
- Identify and assess an organisation's current and future core capabilities required to deliver against business strategy in a competitive operating environment and changing business landscape and economic conditions.
- Articulate organisation's rewards philosophy that includes employee value propositions, non-monetary rewards and performance based monetary rewards to motivate workforce and drive high performance.
- Integrate global best practices in formulating HR strategies to position the organisation as a recognised leader in the industry.
- Evaluate the implications of the external environment (political, regulatory, economic, social, technological, legal) on the industry, organisation and people, and respond to challenges in an agile manner.
- Influence best practices by leveraging lessons learned from HR community and marketplace trends, setting the trend and innovating HR solutions to position the organisation as an employer of choice.
- Scan the local, regional and international business landscape to identify emerging trends and develop long-term strategies, provide insightful perspectives and foresights so as to always take pre-emptive action to capitalise on new opportunities or navigate people-related risk and regulatory complexities.
- Keep pace with the latest trends and developments in HR concepts, tools, techniques, technology and innovative solutions so as to bring insights to enhance value of HR to businesses.
- Provide industry trends and strategic insights across a range of internal business changes and imperatives (dealing with multi-generational workforce, adapting to the changing operating environment, operating effectively across cultures, harnessing innovation, managing digital and technology impact, and transforming HR) to enable senior business leaders and stakeholders to make decisions that help the organisation to be future-ready.
- Actively participate in professional associations, present thought leadership and insights, and share knowledge gained with others through various channels.
- Champion the adoption of workforce analytics (HR metrics and data analytics) as a business intelligence tool that enables sound and informed decision-making around workforce and people management.
- Champion large-scale employee-related or HR initiatives that sets the trend in the industry and enhances the organisation's branding and position as an employer of choice in the industry.
- Provide strategic direction for HR function and initiatives based on insights derived from business performance metrics, HR metrics, and external industry benchmark data.
- Oversee the design and delivery of HR strategy and initiatives and ensure alignment of the HR imperatives to the organisation's strategic goals and business imperatives.
- Position the HR function as a business partner with other business leaders by forging closer relationships and working collaboratively with them to add value to business and employee agenda.
- Serve as a trusted advisor to the business leaders by demonstrating business acumen, industry knowledge and forward thinking HR practices by working through business and operational issues and challenges.
- Provide effective governance and oversight to instil the necessary discipline in policy enforcement and issue resolution.
- Define the impact of current marketplace dynamics and current and future industry trends on the organisation's HR strategy and initiatives prior to implementation.
- Apply a value-based or principle-based approach to dealing with dilemmas or paradoxes and being the "conscience" of the organisation in such situations.
- Advocate the people agenda in all matters to cultivate a culture where employees in the organisation are valued and engaged.
- Help leaders and stakeholders in such a manner that both organisation's and employees interests are protected.
- Engage with members of the board and executive suite level on board-related and organization group-wide issues, such as board member and executive suite member.
- Champion people related initiatives with senior business leaders and stakeholders to promote and enhance employee engagement across the organisation.
- Promote changes to the existing operating environment that positively impacts working relations and partnerships with internal and external stakeholders.
- Formulate the overall learning and development strategy and lifelong learning philosophy through a multi-pronged approach to structured and unstructured learning, coaching, mentoring, and on-the-job training to cultivate a culture of lifelong learning, resilience and change agility.
- Lead HR transformation programmes in pursuit of optimised workforce, HR technology and operational excellence in the delivery of HR services.
- Champion organisation development or change interventions to develop and increase organisational agility and drive high performance culture in the workplace aligning to organisation's strategy and objectives.
- Articulate the impact of changes to employees and change readiness of employees, provide strategic inputs and provide visible support in all communications initiatives to support change interventions and employee engagement initiatives.
- Lead the team in managing complex organizational change and provide insights and guidance to deal with the resulting ambiguity to ensure minimal disruptions to the operations within the organisation and employees are engaged
- Champion organisational values and cultivate desired culture with other senior business leaders and stakeholders.
- Recognise the business implications of upholding ethical principles and proper conduct, and cultivate a highly ethical work environment by holding the HR function to the highest standards of ethical and professional conduct.
- Articulate desired key behaviours premised upon organisational values and desired cultural traits to influence employees to embrace the values and desired culture.
- Identify strategies for managing and resolving organisational challenges in the areas of ethics, culture and performance.
- Steer large scale organisational change or transformation programmes, acting as advisor and change champion.
- Provide clarity to the expected conduct and behaviours of all employees by ensuring behaviours are consistent with the values of the organisation.
